channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Alibaba rolls out AI model, claiming it’s better than DeepSeek-V3
item.description: Chinese tech company Alibaba on Wednesday released a new version of its Qwen 2.5 artificial intelligence model that it claimed surpassed the highly-acclaimed DeepSeek-V3.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg hails Trump administration on Meta earnings call
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Wednesday praised President Trump’s administration and said he looks forward to “redefining” the relationship the tech giant has with governments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need
item.description: The government's personnel shop has devised a five-part strategy for planning, hiring and retaining cybersecurity people. But not everyone follows the strategy. The post How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: SAP: Latest news and insights
item.description: SAP is an enterprise software vendor based in Walldorf, Germany. Its cloud and on-premises enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, including S/4HANA, helps organizations manage their business operations and customer relations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The end of fake VPNs? Google Play Store now shows which VPNs are secure enough to be trusted
item.description: Google's new "Verified" is designed to highlight VPN apps that prioritize user privacy and safety. Here's how it works.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Unlocking the Value of IT Data with Modern Technology
item.description: How AI is transforming data analysis in IT service and operations teams
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI-assisted works can get copyright with enough human creativity, says US copyright office
item.description: Artists can copyright works they made with the help of artificial intelligence, according to a new report by the U.S. Copyright Office that could help clear the way for the use of AI tools in Hollywood, the music industry and other creative fields.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 200 UK companies adopt a permanent four-day work week
item.description: Two years after participating in a groundbreaking four-day work week experiment, 200 companies in the UK have chosen to adopt the policy permanently, marking a significant shift in workplace norms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: NordVPN launches new “revolutionary” censorship-resistant VPN protocol
item.description: NordWhisper is designed to work against the most challenging network filtering techniques. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hideaki Nishino is now the sole CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment, Hermen Hulst remains CEO of PlayStation Studios
item.description: Sony has announced that Hideaki Nishino has been promoted to sole CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment as part of a new leadership restructuring.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: UK government says Microsoft and AWS unfairly dominate the cloud market
item.description: UK Competition and Markets Authority says Microsoft and AWS have an unfair dominance over the country's cloud market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Knowledge graphs: the missing link in enterprise AI
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) are good at learning from unstructured data. But a lot of the proprietary value that enterprises hold is locked up inside relational databases, spreadsheets, and other structured file types.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI poised to overhaul the customer journey
item.description: AI is poised to take over the customer journey for a wide range of products in the next five years. CIOs who ignore the trend risk losing major ground to competitors, AI and marketing experts suggest.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SAP restructures board to emphasize AI-first, suite-first strategy
item.description: SAP is starting the 2025 financial year with a new executive board. The German software company announced that 39-year-old Sebastian Steinhäuser will be promoted to the executive board.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 4 tiny Docker images for lightweight containers
item.description: Docker images can get pretty bulky if you’re not careful. Your typical Docker image includes everything you need to run a particular piece of software (such as the application language’s runtime) and everything those things depend on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Doing authentication right
item.description: Any substantial website is going to need to customize itself for individual users. Thus, it is going to have to authenticate those users — that is, let them log in. As a software developer, it is your job to make the whole process as smooth and secure as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 breaks the sound barrier—the first private aircraft to do so
item.description: About 35,000 feet (10,670 meters) over the Mojave Desert, northwest of Los Angeles, Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 became the first privately funded airplane to break the sound barrier during a test flight on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stable values API would speed Java startups
item.description: Startup times for Java applications would be improved through a proposed API for stable values, which are objects that hold immutable data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system
item.description: The plaintiffs claim OPM violated the E-Government Act by not releasing details of how the email system will manage federal employees’ personal information. The post Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: More than a dozen Jewish groups say they're leaving X amid Musk controversies
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. and Canadian Jewish groups said Tuesday they will leave the social media platform X, stating it "has become rife with toxic speech" amid Elon Musk's changes to the popular social networking app. "More than a dozen U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: America’s answer to Concorde completes its first supersonic flight in a California desert
item.description: Boom Supersonic, the American company building what promises to be the world’s fastest airliner, broke the sound barrier for its first time with a test flight in Mojave.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5
